description:
You are acting as a backup-operator who is double-checking that the Markdown documentation describing the restore procedure is clean before it is shipped to the compliance team.  
The documentation to be checked is already stored in:

/home/user/restore-tests/

Inside that directory you will find several “\*.md” files.  
Your goal is to perform a **single** terminal command that:

1. Recursively scans every “\*.md” file located under /home/user/restore-tests/ (there are only files in that top-level directory right now, but write the command in a recursive-safe way nevertheless).
2. Detects every line that ends with at least one SPACE or TAB character (i.e. “trailing white-space”).
3. Saves all findings into exactly this file (overwrite if it already exists):  
   /home/user/restore-tests/trailing_whitespace.log

Required log-file format (this is what the automated grader will look for):

• One finding per line.  
• Each line must have three colon-separated fields, in this exact order:

   FULL_PATH_TO_FILE:LINE_NUMBER:FULL_TEXT_OF_THE_OFFENDING_LINE

Example of the expected format (do **not** create this line literally; it is only an illustration of the required formatting):

/home/user/restore-tests/example.md:42:A line with two trailing spaces␠␠

Notes & constraints:

• “FULL_PATH_TO_FILE” must be the absolute path (starting with /home/user/).  
• “LINE_NUMBER” must be the 1-based line number reported by the scanning tool.  
• “FULL_TEXT_OF_THE_OFFENDING_LINE” must contain the entire original line including its trailing white-space exactly as it exists in the source file (do **not** strip or add anything).

Use whatever command-line tool(s) you prefer (grep, ripgrep, awk, sed, etc.), **but accomplish everything in exactly one shell command**.  
After the command finishes, verify (for your own confidence) that:

• /home/user/restore-tests/trailing_whitespace.log exists.  
• The file is not empty when at least one offending line was present.  

Once you are satisfied, you are done.
truth:
Initial on-disk state before the agent starts:

Directory structure:
/home/user/restore-tests/
  ├── step1.md
  └── step2.md

File contents:

/home/user/restore-tests/step1.md  (note the two trailing spaces at the end of line 1)
------------------------------------------------------------
Restore Step 1··
Run backup command.··
------------------------------------------------------------
(“·” above represents a space; the real file contains ordinary space characters)

/home/user/restore-tests/step2.md
------------------------------------------------------------
Restore Step 2
Verify backup.
------------------------------------------------------------

Expected file that the agent must create (or overwrite):

/home/user/restore-tests/trailing_whitespace.log
Expected exact content (with two trailing spaces after “Step 1”):

/home/user/restore-tests/step1.md:1:Restore Step 1··

No other lines are expected in the log because only one line in the provided sample files actually ends with trailing white-space.

Permissions:  
All files and directories under /home/user/restore-tests/ are owned by user “user” and are writable by that user; no sudo/root access is required.

The grader will verify:

1. The file /home/user/restore-tests/trailing_whitespace.log exists.  
2. Its contents match exactly the single line shown above, including the two trailing spaces.  
3. No additional data appears in that file.
